description: Pinned CONDITIONS under which the concurrent-TLS-client load harness measures the serving data plane. The SLO target thresholds are declared separately (MCPRE-110, HITL); this file fixes the measurement envelope only. See adr-051-load-harness-envelope.md for prose.
v2_change: v1 measured the deleted object/JCS carrier at concurrency 64 / 2000 requests. v2 pins the RFC 9421 + RFC 9530 serving carrier and raises the canonical load to concurrency 128 / 8000 requests — ONE envelope used by BOTH the local baseline and the GKE fleet run, so they are directly comparable (the earlier GKE run was under-configured at the v1 defaults). Bump MCP_RE_LOADGEN_* to override.

core_count.note: ADR-051 Phase-2 async per-core serving (--features async_serve) IS in effect: the harness spawns the real mcp-re-proxy with --cores N, so the 1->N per-core scaling curve is real. The GKE 2026-07-10 measurements (object/JCS carrier, 64/2000) are SUPERSEDED — see adr-051-slo-targets.json; the production floors are pending re-measurement on GKE under this v2 envelope.

tls_mode.client_auth: required (trusted URI-SAN leaf carrying the RFC 9421 actor_id)

signature_suite.evidence: RFC 9421 HTTP Message Signatures (Ed25519) over covered components incl. RFC 9530 Content-Digest; response signed response-only and bound to the request via ;req. Signature rides in HTTP headers, not a body _meta object.

connection_mode.note: current wire is Connection: close (one request per connection); keepalive realised-reuse fraction is ~0 until Phase-2 keep-alive/H2

reports.per_core_scaling: declared core count + achieved throughput (single point at 1 core on the current single-threaded proxy)

output.machine: JSON written to MCP_RE_LOADGEN_OUT when set
